summary.name: Hierarchical Risk Parity Crypto Diversification Alpha
summary.author: Derek Melchin

summary.description_full:
The strategy targets diversification-driven alpha in liquid crypto by constructing a monthly rebalanced, risk-balanced portfolio of the most actively traded USD-quoted non-stablecoin assets on Coinbase. Each month it forms a universe of the top 10 coins by trailing USD trading volume, then estimates a covariance and correlation structure from daily close-to-close returns over a trailing lookback window (default approximately 12 months). It converts correlations into a distance metric and performs hierarchical clustering, with the premise that correlated coins represent shared risk factors and that allocating risk across decorrelated clusters can improve drawdown control versus naive market-cap or equal-weight baskets.

Portfolio construction uses Hierarchical Risk Parity: assets are ordered by the clustering tree, then weights are assigned via recursive bisection so that higher-variance clusters receive less capital and lower-variance clusters receive more, using inverse-variance estimates within clusters to compute cluster risk. Positions are implemented as long-only target weights across the selected coins, rebalanced at month start; aggregate exposure is scaled to 90 percent of capital (leaving an implicit cash buffer), with no explicit stop-loss, leverage, or momentum/timing overlay. Decisions are made on daily data but only acted on monthly, and the edge is improved risk allocation from clustering-based diversification rather than return forecasting.

raw.name: Hierarchical Risk Parity Crypto Diversification Alpha

author.name: Derek Melchin
author.bio:
Quantitative Developer at QuantConnect. Studied Computer Science and Finance at the University of Lethbridge. Competitor in the 2020-1 Rotman International Trading Competitions. 
See my latest posts at derekmelchin.com.

raw.description:
The strategy targets diversification-driven alpha in liquid crypto by constructing a monthly rebalanced, risk-balanced portfolio of the most actively traded USD-quoted non-stablecoin assets on Coinbase. Each month it forms a universe of the top 10 coins by trailing USD trading volume, then estimates a covariance and correlation structure from daily close-to-close returns over a trailing lookback window (default approximately 12 months). It converts correlations into a distance metric and performs hierarchical clustering, with the premise that correlated coins represent shared risk factors and that allocating risk across decorrelated clusters can improve drawdown control versus naive market-cap or equal-weight baskets.

Portfolio construction uses Hierarchical Risk Parity: assets are ordered by the clustering tree, then weights are assigned via recursive bisection so that higher-variance clusters receive less capital and lower-variance clusters receive more, using inverse-variance estimates within clusters to compute cluster risk. Positions are implemented as long-only target weights across the selected coins, rebalanced at month start; aggregate exposure is scaled to 90 percent of capital (leaving an implicit cash buffer), with no explicit stop-loss, leverage, or momentum/timing overlay. Decisions are made on daily data but only acted on monthly, and the edge is improved risk allocation from clustering-based diversification rather than return forecasting.
